CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-03-2023 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-03-2023 Continued To Mar-29-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Declaration Of Esther Flatto In Support Of Application For Default Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 585(D)
Off Calendar Dec-20-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for Plaintiff's application for default judgment pursuant to CCP 585(D).

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:48:38 a.m. and concluding at 12:10:44 a.m.

Plaintiff Esther B. Flatto, in Individual appearing in pro per, is placed under oath, and testifies via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Betabrand Corporation, a Delaware Corporation not appearing.

Plaintiff presents facts of case and is examined by the Court.
The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 2 - Letter dated November 21, 2017 addressed to Joshua A. Ridless, Esq.

Court further examines Plaintiff regarding its request for restitution, damages, and its out of pocket costs.

The matter is submitted.
Court finds Plantiff has proven all elements to support its second amended complaint.
Based on the documents filed, the statements made by Plaintiff, consideration and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Esther B. Flatto, and against Defendant Betabrand Corporation, A Delaware Corporation, as follows:

1. Unpaid Wages: $600,000.00
2. Damages (10% of Revenue): $138,149.50

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $738,149.50

Plaintiff is self-represented and cannot recover attorney fees pursuant to Civil Code of Procedure 1033.5(a)(10). Plaintiff's request for attorney fees is DENIED.

Plaintiff's request for restitution based on revenue is DENIED.

Plaintiff shall calculate interest.
Proposed judgment to be submitted.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-17-2022 Continued To Jan-25-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Declaration Of Esther Flatto In Support Of Application For Default Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 585(D)
Off Calendar Nov-10-2022 Continued To Dec-20-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for Plaintiff's application for default judgment.

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:04:40 a.m. and concluding at 10:12:43 a.m.

Plaintiff Esther B. Flatto appearing in pro per, is placed under oath, and testies.
Defendant Betabrand Corporation, a Delaware Corporation not appearing.

Plaintiff presents offer of proof.
Court is unable to determine the requests of Plaintiff.
Court shall research this matter to determine Plaintiff's request for damages, interest, attorney fees, and costs.

Court orders matter continued on its own motion to December 20, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514.

DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:

Self-represented plaintiff, Esther B. Flatto ( ) appears on her own behalf as opposing party

Attorney, CHRISTOPHER S. SUN (Email: csun@keker.com) of the law offices of KEKER, VAN NEST & PETERS LLP (Tel: 415-391 5400) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

On calendar:
DEFENDANT BETABRAND CORPORATION DEMURRER to SECOND Amended COMPLAINT

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides on record as reflected in the notes of the Certified Shorthand Reporter.

DEFENDANT BETABRAND CORPORATION's DEMURRER to Second Amended COMPLAINT is TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION by Judge Richard B. Ulmer on Jan-22-2021.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

***After the hearing, the Court rules via written order. DEFENDANT BETABRAND CORPORATION's DEMURRER to Second Amended COMPLAINT is in part sustained without leave to amend and in part overruled. Written order is signed.***

DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-13-2021 Continued To Jan-22-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:

Esther Flatto In Propria Persona, appears as self-represented plaintiff.

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT BETABRAND CORPORATION's DEMURRER to SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is CONTINUED to January 22, 2021 at 9:30 AM in Department 302, to be heard by Judge Richard Ulmer.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-09-2020 Continued To May-06-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT BETABRAND CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Esther Flatto (415-260-4787) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Christopher S. Sun, esq. (415-962-8805) of Keker Van Nest & Peters for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Betabrand Corporation's demurrers to the following causes of action in Esther Flatto's first amended complaint (FAC):

1&2 (Breach of Fiduciary Duty - Undivided Loyalty, Confidentiality). Flatto pleads that she and Betabrand were "fiduciaries" because they "were in a joint venture." (FAC 19:18-19.) However, as Betabrand notes, and Flatto concedes, an essential element of a joint venture is that the venturers share losses, and Flatto does not so plead. (Memo. 4:9-14, citing Cislaw v. Southland Corp. (1992) 4 Cal.App.4th 1284, 1297; Opp. 9:8-14, citing CACI 3712.) Based on her repeated failures to plead a fiduciary duty cause of action (and on what she has pled), it is apparent that Flatto cannot plead one, so these demurrers are sustained without leave to amend.

3 (Constructive Fraud). This cause of action too requires a fiduciary duty, which, as held above, Flatto has not pled. (Memo. 5:18-21, citing Prakashpalan v. Engstrom, Lipscomb & Lack (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 1105, 1131.) Moreover, Flatto fails to adequately plead reliance on any misrepresentation. (See Memo. 7:2-16.) Based on her repeated failures to plead (and on what she has pled), it is apparent that Flatto cannot plead this cause of action, so the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

5&6 (UCL). The FAC's allegations in these causes of action, while not models of clarity, suffice at the pleading stage. Betabrand's demurrers are overruled as to these two causes.

8 (Promissory Estoppel). The FAC's allegations suffice at the pleading stage; this demurrer is overruled.

New Causes of Action. The FAC has three new causes of action - 4, 7 and 9 - not included in Flatto's original complaint and not addressed above. Flatto did not obtain permission to add new causes, so these are stricken. (Community Water Coalition v. Santa Cruz County Local Agency Formation Com. (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 1317, 1329.) If Flatto wishes to plead additional causes of action, she may file a noticed motion seeking leave to do so.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-03-2020 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Christopher S. Sun, Esq. of KEKER VAN NEST & PETERS, T:415.962.8805, for defendant/moving party;
Esther Flatto, T:415.260.4787, plaintiff appearing on her own behalf, by Court Call.

RULING: Prior to the hearing, the Court provided the parties with a further tentative ruling. Plaintiff in pro per argued the tentative ruling. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT BETABRAND CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendant Betabrand Corporation's demurrer to the complaint is sustained with leave to amend. The demurrer was filed and served two and a half months ago - on October 21, 2019. I gave plaintiff Esther Flatto additional time to respond to the demurrer - through December 26, 2019 - but she failed to do so.

The Court to prepare an order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Order signed after hearing.
